name: Rouladen (Rolmups)
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Top Round Steak, 1/4 inch Thick
3 Dill Pickles, Rinsed and Halved Lengthwise
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound steak to 1/4 inch thickness.
2. Cut into 6 rectangular pieces about 4 inches wide and 8 inches long.
3. Spread each piece with a teaspoon of mustard and sprinkle with 2 teaspoons onion.
4. Place a strip of bacon down the center.
5. Place half of a pickle across the short end of the rectangle.
6. Roll meat up jellyroll style around the pickle to form a cylinder.
7. Tie rolls at each end with kitchen cord.
8. Heat vegetable oil in a heavy 10 to 12 inch skillet over medium-high.
9. Add beef rolls and brown on all sides, regulating heat so they color quickly and evenly without burning.
10. Transfer rolls to a plate; pour water into the skillet and bring to a boil, scraping any brown particles from pan bottom and sides.
11. Add celery, leeks, parsnips, parsley, and salt; return beef rolls to skillet.
12. Cover; reduce heat to low and simmer 1 hour, or until meat shows no resistance when pierced with a fork. Turn rolls once or twice during cooking period.
13. Transfer rolls to heated platter; cover with foil to keep warm.
14. Strain cooking liquid through fine sieve; measure and return to skillet; boil briskly until reduced to 2 cups; remove from heat.
15. Melt butter in small saucepan over moderate heat; when foam subsides, sprinkle in flour; lower heat and cook, stirring constantly, until flour turns golden brown.
16. Gradually add reduced cooking liquid, beating vigorously with whisk until sauce is smooth and thick.
17. Taste for seasoning; return sauce and rolls to skillet; simmer only long enough to reheat rolls through.
18. Serve rolls on heated platter and pour sauce over them. Rouladen are often served with red cabbage and dumplings or boiled potatoes.
